L1.area: Sandbox code execution and expression evaluation logic

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where the return values of function calls executed within the sandboxed environment are not properly sanitized. Arrays returned by sandboxed function calls can contain references to privileged constructors from the host environment. An attacker can leverage these leaked references to construct objects that bypass sandbox restrictions, ultimately achieving arbitrary code execution on the host system.

L3.area: Sandbox function call dispatch and return value handling in the expression evaluator
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where arrays returned from function calls executed within the sandbox are not sanitized for references to privileged host constructors. By invoking built-in methods that produce arrays containing object introspection results, attacker-controlled sandboxed code can obtain a reference to the host Function constructor and use it to execute arbitrary code outside the sandbox.

ground_truth.reason: The sandbox does not sanitize arrays returned from function calls, allowing attacker-controlled sandboxed code to obtain the host Function constructor via methods like Object.entries() or Object.values(), enabling sandbox escape and arbitrary command execution.
